title: Is there a God
wikitext:
[]: Is there a God?             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Thu Jul 21, 2005 1:56 am Anybody else find it odd that, inspite of all the religeous references, we never actually see or hear from the big guy Himself in Eva? I mean, there is the Angels, but those don't necessarily prove that the original Eva universe has a God. Since they hatched out of a giant egg from space, they could just be garden variety space aliens. Perhaps they inspired the Angels mentioned in the Bible. You know, that whole thing about gods & mythical creatures being based on ancient peoples' accounts of alien sightings. Then there's all that religeous stuff with SEELE, but that's not proof, either. Powerful madmen have a long history of talking about God to justify their evil schemes. So, what do you think? Is there a tangible God in the world of Eva, or are all the stange & horrible events the work of space monsters & the lunatics who want to manipulate them?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2005 6:52 am The world of NGE is one without "the big G". Nothing is truly mystical or to be taken upon by faith alone. It all boils down to cold, hard technology, and using that technology in ways that we don't completely understand. When souls can be manipulated by machines and gods can be grown in labs, nothing is really sacred.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:33 am If there is a God in Eva (which I don't think there is) it is far removed from the events of the day. The Angels are simply other-worldy beings, not necessarily disciples and cotemoraries of a God. They are like man, simply another form of life that exists in the Universe. How and why they got here is a mystery (althought the Eva 2 game apparently clears most of that up--and I would have to say that I don't see anything wrong with its explanation--it was along lines of what I was thinking when watching Eva). God never interjects him/herself into the action, at least not in anyway known to the actors. Having God lingering around the world of Eva doesn't mean much IMO for the storyline. I think Reichu said it nicely--its about men using technology in ways that manipulate the soul (whatever that is), manipulating it ways that are bad news...

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:46 am I'm reminded of the Tleilaxu in the Dune boks, who refer to DNA as "the language of God".

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu Jul 21, 2005 11:29 pm bp32 wrote: The Angels are simply other-worldy beings, not necessarily disciples and contemporaries of a God. They are like man, simply another form of life that exists in the Universe.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 22, 2005 12:51 am Tenshi means messenger, actually (it's a translation of angelos ). Shito means apostle or disciple. And I should point out, neither shito nor Angel is "proper", since they are BOTH used in the original show. I would hesitate to call the Angels "otherworldly", since most of them were born on the same rock as us.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Fri Jul 22, 2005 2:11 am Reichu wrote: Tenshi means messenger, actually (it's a translation of angelos ). Shito means apostle or disciple. And I should point out, neither shito nor Angel is "proper", since they are BOTH used in the original show.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Jul 22, 2005 4:18 am Reichu wrote: Tenshi means messenger, actually (it's a translation of angelos ).

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 22, 2005 5:27 am ^ I do think so, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 22, 2005 6:40 am That's what I get for posting late at night. I guess it's like "heavenly messenger" or something. And, uh, shito is "messenger-follower", or, uh, something. It's the Japanese translation of Apostle/Disciple.

[]:             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Fri Jul 22, 2005 7:28 pm Reichu wrote: Tenshi means messenger, actually (it's a translation of angelos ). Shito means apostle or disciple. And I should point out, neither shito nor Angel is "proper", since they are BOTH used in the original show. I would hesitate to call the Angels "otherworldly", since most of them were born on the same rock as us.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 22, 2005 7:38 pm The Angels (Sachiel thru Armisael) originated in some unknown manner from their 'mother', Adam, at some indeterminate time after she arrived on Earth via the White Moon (or Egg of Adam). They were all born on Earth, and are thus designated Terran citizens. It is Adam and Lilith who are extraterrestrial in origin.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Sat Jul 23, 2005 7:02 pm Yes, technically they are both of the Earth--by otherwordly I meant that their origins are technically not indigenous to the Earth--both humans and Angels are the offspring of otherwordly beings that happened to both end up on this planet--so in that sense they are (as are humans) otherworldy. But one could argue they are both "first-generation" earthlings :)

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 23, 2005 7:09 pm Yes their is a god in EVA, his name is ANNO.

[]:             Quote Post by bp32 » Sat Jul 23, 2005 7:12 pm DatDude wrote: Yes their is a god in EVA, his name is ANNO.

[]:             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Sun Jul 24, 2005 1:41 am Reichu wrote: The Angels (Sachiel thru Armisael) originated in some unknown manner from their 'mother', Adam, at some indeterminate time after she arrived on Earth via the White Moon (or Egg of Adam). They were all born on Earth, and are thus designated Terran citizens. It is Adam and Lilith who are extraterrestrial in origin.

[]: Re: Is there a God?             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jul 24, 2005 2:56 am Iblis 19th Angel wrote: Anybody else find it odd that, inspite of all the religeous references, we never actually see or hear from the big guy Himself in Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 24, 2005 3:18 am Adam and Lilith were created expressly for the purpose of propagating life: Life from one holds the Fruit of Life, the other holds the Fruit of Knowledge. The video game "NGE 2" contains data files holding 'secrets' apparently gleaned from Anno himself... It's said pretty straight-up that Adam and Lil were just part of a larger production line, as it were, by some self-righteous alien race who felt that it was their duty to populate the galaxy by sending out these 'Seeds of Life'. For the skeptical, hints are in the show itself: Gendo tells Fuyutsuki that the empty space within the Geofront was excavated by 'someone else', and the neutrino scan of the White Moon says the 'surface structure is clearly artificial'.

[]:             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Sun Jul 24, 2005 5:16 am Reichu wrote: Adam and Lilith were created expressly for the purpose of propagating life: Life from one holds the Fruit of Life, the other holds the Fruit of Knowledge. The video game "NGE 2" contains data files holding 'secrets' apparently gleaned from Anno himself... It's said pretty straight-up that Adam and Lil were just part of a larger production line, as it were, by some self-righteous alien race who felt that it was their duty to populate the galaxy by sending out these 'Seeds of Life'. For the skeptical, hints are in the show itself: Gendo tells Fuyutsuki that the empty space within the Geofront was excavated by 'someone else', and the neutrino scan of the White Moon says the 'surface structure is clearly artificial'.

[]:             Quote Post by Hiromakuta » Sat Sep 10, 2005 7:39 am Seele killed God...

 Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Sun Jul 24, 2005 1:41 am Reichu wrote: The Angels (Sachiel thru Armisael) originated in some unknown manner from their 'mother', Adam, at some indeterminate time after she arrived on Earth via the White Moon (or Egg of Adam). They were all born on Earth, and are thus designated Terran citizens. It is Adam and Lilith who are extraterrestrial in origin.

[]: Re: Is there a God?             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jul 24, 2005 2:56 am Iblis 19th Angel wrote: Anybody else find it odd that, inspite of all the religeous references, we never actually see or hear from the big guy Himself in Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 24, 2005 3:18 am Adam and Lilith were created expressly for the purpose of propagating life: Life from one holds the Fruit of Life, the other holds the Fruit of Knowledge. The video game "NGE 2" contains data files holding 'secrets' apparently gleaned from Anno himself... It's said pretty straight-up that Adam and Lil were just part of a larger production line, as it were, by some self-righteous alien race who felt that it was their duty to populate the galaxy by sending out these 'Seeds of Life'. For the skeptical, hints are in the show itself: Gendo tells Fuyutsuki that the empty space within the Geofront was excavated by 'someone else', and the neutrino scan of the White Moon says the 'surface structure is clearly artificial'.
